name: Sabre Firebird
playstyle: Hit-and-run missile strikes. Approach fast, dump missiles, disengage before a furball develops. Not a dogfighter — a delivery system.

builds.label: PvE Strike
builds.notes: Fire all 12 missiles on approach then guns to finish. Massive opening burst. Reload at station between runs. FR-66 shield BP from Foxwell Priority is the key upgrade.

builds.label: PvP Ambush
builds.notes: Approach low-signature, dump Tempests, follow with Panther burst. Very hard to counter if the alpha lands. Fragile if they survive the opening.

verdict: ⚠ Pledge only in 4.8 — no aUEC purchase. Exceptional missile opener but thin on sustained fights. Works best in org operations with backup.

name: Meteor
playstyle: One-pass brawler. The 2× bespoke S5 Leonid cannons are locked — cannot be swapped. Build the S3 support slots and components around them. Fly for clean passes, not sustained dogfights.

slots.label: Guns (locked)
slots.val: 2× S5 Leonid Ballistic Cannon (bespoke — cannot swap)

builds.label: PvE Deadbolt Build
builds.weapons: 4× Deadbolt III (S3 ballistic) + locked S5 Leonids
builds.missiles: Ignite IV (S4) × 4 + S2 Ignites for fixed racks
builds.notes: The Leonids strip armor, Deadbolts finish. Highest alpha burst of any medium fighter. Open with all missiles then Leonids + Deadbolts simultaneously. Devastating on ERTs.

builds.label: PvP Mixed Build
builds.weapons: 2× CF-337 Panther + 2× M3A Laser (S3) + locked S5 Leonids
builds.notes: Mixed energy sustains better in extended PvP. Panther + Laser combo is versatile vs all target types. Still flies pass-based — do not try to out-turn a Gladius.

verdict: 4.8 meta pick for org ops and ERT farming. Best alpha damage of any solo medium fighter. The Leonids alone justify running it. Pledge only currently.

name: F7C-S Hornet Ghost Mk II
playstyle: Low-signature combat and reconnaissance. Stealth QD lets you arrive cold. Not as fast as the Sabre but more survivable and practical for solo daily use.

builds.label: Stealth PvP Build
builds.qd: Spectre S1 or Zephyr S1 (stealth QD)
builds.notes: Arrive low-signature, open with missiles + Deadbolt burst. Size 4 guns punch well above this ship's weight. Stealth components keep EM below most scanner thresholds. Avoid sustained fights.

builds.label: PvE Budget Build
builds.notes: Laser cannons for sustained HRT/VHRT PvE. Ghost's lower signature means fewer ganks from other players mid-mission. Good daily driver if you prioritise not being a target.

verdict: PvE meta: solid daily driver. PvP: high skill ceiling — works when you control the engagement, punishing when you don't. Stealth QD is the real feature.

name: Golem
playstyle: Solo mining. Budget Prospector alternative. Best on soft/medium rocks — avoid high-resistance materials. Mining laser is the main tool, the 2× S1 guns are purely defensive.

builds.label: Balanced Mining Build
builds.missiles: None — use that weight budget elsewhere
builds.notes: Keep mining laser at Optimum extraction rate — don't overdrive on tough rocks. Best targets: Janalite, Hadanite, Quantainium (soft veins only). Refine before selling.

verdict: Best entry mining ship in 4.8. Cheaper than Prospector at similar solo output on easy rocks. Not a replacement for the MOLE or ATLS GEO for gem mining — know your role.

name: Eclipse
playstyle: Capital ship assassination. Approach under stealth, fire 3× S9 torpedoes, disengage immediately. This is not a fighter — do not try to dogfight in it. One job, done perfectly.

slots.label: Torpedoes
slots.val: 3× S9 torpedo hardpoints (cannot be swapped to guns)

builds.label: Full Stealth Assassination
builds.notes: Arrester IIIs are the go-to S9 torpedoes — slow but massive damage. Approach from the blind spot, fire before target radar locks you. All 3 torps on a capital = instant structural damage. S2 guns are for desperate defense only — disengage if found.

verdict: Org siege weapon. Devastating against Hammerheads, Idris, and large player ships. Useless in a furball. The Catfight approach: Eclipse escorts advance, drops torps on the flagship, retreats.

name: Mantis
playstyle: Quantum enforcement. Pull ships out of QT with the QED module, then either destroy them or hold for org backup. Best used with a combat escort — the Mantis wins fights by starting them on your terms.

slots.label: QED Module
slots.val: Quantum Enforcement Device (core feature — cannot remove)

slots.label: Guns
slots.val: 2× S3 pilot + 2× S3 remote turret (4× S3 total)

builds.label: Org Interdiction Build
builds.missiles: Ignite III (S3) × 4 — opening burst on pulled target
builds.notes: QED pulls target out of QT. Immediately fire missiles + laser burst on the disoriented ship. Have a Gladius or Arrow in the org ready to clean up. Range is ~20km for the pull — position ahead of the jump lane.

builds.label: Piracy / PvP Build
builds.notes: Deadbolts strip armor on pulled target fast. Remote turret adds significant sustained DPS for a solo pilot. Works solo but much better with an org escort covering your flanks while you pull.

verdict: Unique tactical asset. The QED makes it irreplaceable for org PvP and piracy. Underused — most pilots don't know how to position for a QT pull. Learn the lane geometry and it's a game-changer.

name: Gladius
playstyle: The best ship to learn dogfighting. Forgiving, fast, dual engines. 3× S3 fixed pilot guns. Low barrier to entry — punches above its price.

slots.label: Missiles
slots.val: 1× S3 rack + 2× S2 racks (6 missiles total)

builds.label: PvE Deadbolt Build
builds.notes: Best PvE setup. Deadbolts strip NPC armor fast. FR-66 shield BP from Foxwell Priority is priority #1 upgrade. Run this until you can afford the F7C.

builds.label: PvP Fixed Laser Build
builds.notes: Energy sustain is better for extended PvP. M3A has tight convergence on the Gladius nose — easy to land. Learn to lead targets at range.

verdict: Best first combat ship. Dual engines = survivability. Once you can afford the F7C Mk II upgrade, do it — but master the Gladius first.

name: Arrow
playstyle: Fastest light fighter. Single S1 shield — fragile. High skill ceiling. Rewards fixed-weapon accuracy. Fly like a knife, not a brawler.

builds.label: Full Deadbolt Fixed
builds.notes: Max burst damage. Deadbolts are unforgiving but hit hard. S1 shield means you cannot trade hits — dodge everything. Best at HRT tier, struggles at VHRT solo.

verdict: Cheapest viable combat ship at 1.1M aUEC. Great stepping stone to the Gladius. Do not try to tank anything — you have one shield. Speed is your armor.

name: Buccaneer
playstyle: Glass cannon. Highest alpha burst of any light fighter. 240 SCM. One S1 shield — even thinner than the Arrow. Get the first hit in or die.

slots.label: Guns
slots.val: 1× S4 ventral (fixed) + 2× S3 chin (fixed) + 2× S1 nose (fixed)

builds.label: Full Deadbolt Alpha Build
builds.weapons: Deadbolt IV (S4) + 2× Deadbolt III (S3) + 2× Deadbolt I (S1)
builds.shield: Asclepius S1 — lightest you can run
builds.notes: All ballistic fixed. Fire missiles immediately on approach then dump all guns simultaneously. The convergence takes practice — S4 ventral angles differently to S3 chin guns. At HRT tier this is devastating. At VHRT you will die if they survive the opener.

verdict: Best budget PvE alpha striker. If you can aim fixed weapons and tolerate dying occasionally, this punches above its price. Do not fly it like a Gladius.

name: F7C Hornet Mk II
playstyle: Org workhorse. Flexible medium fighter with S3/S4/S5 hardpoints. Upgrade the S5 centre slot first — it is the highest-value slot on the ship.

slots.label: Guns
slots.val: 1× S3 nose (fixed) + 2× S4 wing (fixed) + 1× S5 centre (fixed) + 2× S1 chin turret (remote)

builds.label: PvE Meta — Mixed Deadbolt
builds.weapons: Deadbolt V (S5) + 2× Deadbolt IV (S4) + M3A (S3 nose)
builds.missiles: Ignite III × 8 — open ERTs with full salvo
builds.notes: The S5 Deadbolt V is the cornerstone — highest single-gun damage in the loadout. M3A on nose for energy sustain between reloads. FR-76 shield BP from Foxwell Red tier. Best ERT farming ship at this price bracket.

builds.label: Full Energy Sustain
builds.notes: No ammo management. Good for long sessions stacking 10+ ERT contracts. Lower peak DPS than the Deadbolt build but never runs dry.

verdict: The org daily driver. Upgrade path: Arrow → Gladius → F7C Mk II. Once you have this, the F7C-M is the obvious next step for duo play.

name: F7C-M Super Hornet Mk II
playstyle: 2-seat F7C. Gunner in the ball turret adds sustained DPS. 3× S1 shields vs F7C's 2. Best duo combat ship in 4.8. Fly solo in a pinch but you lose the turret advantage.

slots.label: Guns (pilot)
slots.val: 1× S3 nose + 2× S4 wing + 1× S5 centre (all fixed)

slots.label: Missiles
slots.val: 4× S3 racks + 2× S1 racks (16 missiles total)

builds.label: Duo Org Build
builds.weapons: Deadbolt V (S5) + 2× Deadbolt IV (S4) + M3A (S3) | turret: 2× CF-007 Bulldog (S1)
builds.notes: Gunner runs the ball turret on smaller fighters that get inside your turning radius. Pilot handles ERTs, gunner handles anything that flanks. The 3-shield setup is noticeably tankier than the F7C. Communicate target priority.

verdict: Best duo ship in 4.8 under 10M aUEC. If you and a regular partner both own one, you can run ERT stacks all session and rarely die.

name: Sabre
playstyle: Best alpha burst of any medium fighter. 4× S3 fixed guns all converging on one point. Run stealth components for full effect. Levski only — Teach's Ship Shop.

builds.label: Full Deadbolt Alpha
builds.notes: 4× Deadbolt III simultaneously is the highest S3-level burst in the game. Opens fights with missiles + full ballistic burst. Devastating at all NPC tiers. The S1 shield is thinner than the F7C — do not brawl, pick your shots.

builds.label: Stealth PvP
builds.notes: Full stealth stack — low EM arrival, Panthers keep EM low in combat too. Arrive cold, open with Tempests, follow with 4× Panther burst. Exceptional ambush capability.

verdict: Best S3 alpha striker. Stealth is a genuine combat advantage in 4.8. Levski-only purchase makes it less accessible but worth the trip.

name: Scorpius
playstyle: Best value heavy fighter. 9 weapon hardpoints across pilot + turret. Massive shield for a medium frame. 2-crew advantage for sustained fights — gunner handles flankers.

slots.label: Guns (pilot)
slots.val: 4× S3 body (fixed) + 3× S4 racks — 7 pilot hardpoints

builds.label: Org ERT Build
builds.weapons: 3× Deadbolt IV (S4 rack) + 4× Deadbolt III (S3 body) | turret: 2× M3A S3
builds.notes: 7 pilot guns firing simultaneously is enormous burst. S4 rack guns are the priority upgrade. Gunner on the rear turret cleans up anything that gets behind you. Stack 10+ ERT contracts — the shield soaks enough hits to run a long session.

verdict: Underrated 4.8 pick. 10,560 HP shield on a medium frame is exceptional. If you have a regular gunner partner this is the best combat investment under 10M.

name: Vanguard Harbinger
playstyle: ERT specialist. Dual M2 shields = 21,120 HP — extremely tanky for a solo ship. Bed bay lets you log out mid-session. S5 nose is the primary weapon. Park near the fight, work contracts methodically.

slots.label: Missiles
slots.val: 3× S5 racks + 2× S3 racks + 4× S2 racks (large missile package)

builds.label: ERT Solo Grind
builds.weapons: Deadbolt V (S5 nose) + 4× CF-007 (S2 chin) + rear: 2× CF-007 S1
builds.missiles: Ignite V × 3 + Ignite III × 6 + Ignite II × 8
builds.notes: Deadbolt V is the S5 priority. The 4× S2 chin guns sustain between nose reloads. Open every ERT with the full missile package — 3× S5 Ignites alone can kill most targets. FR-76 shield BPs from Foxwell Red tier are the best upgrade for this ship.

verdict: Best solo ERT ship in 4.8. The dual M2 shields mean you rarely die. The bed means you log out at the fight location. At 10.93M it is a serious investment — worth every aUEC.

name: Constellation Andromeda
playstyle: Best solo ERT + haul combo. 4× S5 fixed pilot guns + massive missile package. 96 SCU cargo — loot every kill. Slower than fighters but the firepower and shield make up for it.

slots.label: Missiles
slots.val: 24× S2 + 28× S1 racks — massive opening salvo

builds.label: ERT Loot Build
builds.weapons: 4× Deadbolt V (S5 fixed) | turrets: 2× M3A S3
builds.missiles: Full rack — open every ERT with everything. 4× S5 Deadbolts + 52 missiles simultaneously is devastating.
builds.notes: Open with ALL missiles on ERT approach — 52 missiles is your opener. Then 4× Deadbolt V sustained fire. 96 SCU cargo bay: loot components and pilot suits after each kill. Can make 300-500k per ERT on top of contract pay. Slow to accelerate — stay in the fight, do not try to chase.

verdict: Solo ERT grind + passive loot income. The Connie does everything. Crew the turrets with org members for multiplied effectiveness.

name: Hammerhead
playstyle: Org anti-fighter platform. 6 manned S4 turrets. Needs full crew to be worth it. Devastating with 6 gunners. A ghost town without them. Coordinate turret sectors — do not all shoot the same target.

slots.label: Turrets
slots.val: 6× manned turrets (2× S4 guns each = 12× S4 total)

builds.label: Org Fleet Build
builds.missiles: Ignite III × 16 — ship captain fires these on approach
builds.notes: Assign turret sectors: 2 gunners forward, 2 mid, 2 rear. Call targets on comms — do not all shoot the same ship. The Hammerhead melts fighter waves when coordinated. The captain handles navigation and missile fire. Assign a co-pilot for shields and power management.

verdict: Org siege weapon. Useless solo. With 5 gunners it is the most effective anti-fighter platform in the game. Reserve this for Catfight fleet ops.

name: Shiv
playstyle: 4.8 breakout ship. Heavy firepower + 32 SCU cargo + bed + medium shield. Does everything. Best solo all-rounder in 4.8. Levski / Lorville.

slots.label: Missiles
slots.val: 2× S3 racks + 4× S2 racks (10 missiles total)

builds.label: Solo All-rounder
builds.weapons: 2× Deadbolt IV (S4 pilot fixed) + 2× M3A S3 (remote turret)
builds.notes: S4 Deadbolts on pilot guns strip armor fast. M3A remote turret adds sustained energy DPS. 32 SCU cargo: loot every kill you make — at ERT tier components are worth 5-50k each. Bed for log-out anywhere. The most versatile 5M investment in 4.8.

builds.label: Duo Combat
builds.weapons: 2× Deadbolt IV (S4) + gunner: 2× Deadbolt III (S3 turret)
builds.notes: Gunner on the rear turret handles flankers. The combined DPS of pilot + gunner rivals ships costing 3× as much. At VHRT/ERT tier this is a serious ship.

verdict: The 4.8 value king. 5.5M aUEC for a ship that fights, hauls, and has a bed. If you own one ship this patch, make it the Shiv.

name: Perseus
playstyle: Org capital investment. 4× S7 manned turrets + 2× S5 pilot guns. 10× S5 missiles. Anti-capital platform. Needs near-full crew to justify the cost.

builds.label: Org Flagship Build
builds.weapons: 2× M5A S5 pilot + 4× turrets: Deadbolt VII (S7) each
builds.notes: Assign 4 gunners to turrets, 1 co-pilot for power/shields, 1 on comms/navigation. S7 turrets are capital-ship weapons — they shred Hammerheads. Open with all 10× S5 missiles on approach then sustained turret fire. The 211,200 HP shield means it tanks incredible punishment. This is Catfight's siege ship.

verdict: The org's biggest gun. 39.68M is a collective investment — treat it like one. Do not fly it solo. Designate a regular crew and run org fleet ops.

name: Asgard
playstyle: 4.8 breakout heavy fighter. 4× S4 + 2× S3 pilot guns. 16× S3 missiles. M2 shield on a single-seat ship. Designed for sustained solo combat at VHRT/ERT tier.

slots.label: Missiles
slots.val: 16× S3 racks — largest light missile package in class

builds.label: ERT Sustained Build
builds.missiles: 16× Ignite III — open every ERT with full salvo, then guns
builds.notes: 16 missiles is an absurd opener for a single-seat ship. Fire all on approach then 4× S4 + 2× S3 sustained. The M shield means you survive engagements the Vanguard Harbinger would too but with more guns. Good for stacking 8-10 ERT contracts solo.

builds.label: Bunker / Ground Op Support
builds.weapons: Same as above + load Medical Ursa in cargo bay (fits)
builds.notes: Community-found: Medical Ursa fits in the cargo bay. Fly to bunker, drop the Ursa outside, run the bunker, respawn on the Ursa if killed. Massive survivability upgrade for solo bunker runs.

verdict: Best new solo heavy fighter in 4.8. Expensive at 17.86M but earns it back fast at ERT tier. The Medical Ursa trick makes it exceptional for bunker ops.

name: Carrack
playstyle: Best exploration ship. Long quantum range, repair drones, snub fighter bay, 456 SCU cargo. Org expedition flagship. Not a combat ship — but has defensive turrets.

slots.label: QD
slots.val: 1× L Quantum Drive — longest range in game

builds.label: Exploration Fit
builds.weapons: Defensive turrets: M3A S3 energy — long sustain, no ammo
builds.qd: ISYSTEM Pronto L or Atlas L — prioritise range
builds.notes: The QD range is the Carrack's primary weapon. Equip the longest-range quantum drive available. Carry a Pisces in the snub bay for surface scouting. Repair drones handle hull damage in the field. Assign a dedicated shield/power operator for combat encounters.

verdict: Org expedition ship. Not for combat grinding — for long-range ops, Pyro deep runs, and exploration content. The 456 SCU cargo makes it self-sufficient on long deployments.

name: Vulture
playstyle: Best solo salvage ship. Hull strip + component salvage. Bring Cambio SRT canisters. Stable 4.8 income. Not a combat ship — the S1 defensive gun is for emergencies only.

builds.label: RMC Farming Build
builds.notes: No weapon upgrade needed — put budget into shield and QD. Cambio SRT canisters for component stripping are the priority consumable. Hull strip derelicts for RMC, strip components for direct sell. Avoid player zones — you cannot fight back. Run Adagio Titanium haul missions between salvage sessions for income diversity.

verdict: Cheap entry to salvage income. 1.9M is easy to earn at HRT tier and pays itself back quickly. RSI Salvation is now a cheaper alternative at 1.19M if you want to start salvage faster.

name: Reclaimer
playstyle: Fastest RMC per hour with full crew. Not worth flying solo. Needs 3 beam operators minimum + 1 coordinator. 194,400 HP shield — nearly unkillable. Role-specific ship.

builds.label: Full Crew RMC Build
builds.weapons: Turrets: M3A S3 energy (defensive, no ammo management)
builds.shield: Asclepius C (whatever is available at C-grade)
builds.notes: Crew roles: 1 pilot/coordinator, 2 beam operators (front beams), 1 tractor beam operator (component pull), 1 turret gunner for escorts. Coordinator calls targets on comms. Beam operators work different sections of the hull simultaneously. Component stripper prioritises shields > power plants > quantum drives (highest sell value). With 4 active crew this generates 700k-1M+ aUEC/hr in RMC alone.

verdict: Org salvage endgame. Solo = waste of a Reclaimer. With 4-5 crew it is unmatched. Designate a regular Reclaimer crew within Catfight.

name: Prospector
playstyle: Solo asteroid mining standard. Target Quantainium (Yela/Cellin) for max value. Always refine before selling. S2 power + S2 cooler despite small frame — good thermal management.

slots.label: Power
slots.val: 1× M Power Plant (note: M-grade in a small ship)

builds.label: Quantainium Optimised
builds.notes: Mining laser upgrades are the priority — not weapons. Hofstede S2 or Lancet S2 mining heads for better rock cracking. Surge modifier for power spike on tough rock faces. Target Quantainium veins — extract at 30-35% charge, never push to 100%. Always refine at Lorville/Port Tressler — raw ore sells for 30-40% less. Yela/Cellin asteroid belt for Quantainium density.

verdict: The solo mining benchmark. 2.1M aUEC is very achievable early. ATLS GEO has replaced it for gem mining but Prospector still dominates asteroid Quantainium runs.

name: MOLE
playstyle: Best group mining ship. 3 independent mining lasers — each operator works their own rock face. Quantainium split 3 ways still beats solo Prospector. Glaciem Ring (Nyx) for best ore quality.

slots.label: Mining Lasers
slots.val: 3× independent S2 mining heads (one per operator position)

builds.label: 3-Crew Quantainium Build
builds.weapons: Leave stock — focus budget on mining heads
builds.notes: Each of the 3 positions works independently. Coordinator (pilot position) calls rock targets and monitors ship power. The other 2 operators crack their assigned rocks simultaneously. Communication is everything — call the charge %, who has the surge modifier, and when to back off. Full load of Quantainium = 300-400k each after refinery. Glaciem Ring in Nyx system has higher ore density and quality than Stanton asteroid belts.

verdict: The org mining flagship. If Catfight wants to run a mining session, MOLE + 3 crew is the standard. Assign permanent crew positions for efficiency.

name: F8C Lightning
playstyle: Best solo heavy fighter in 4.8 if you can get one. 6 pilot guns (2× S3 + 4× S2). Smaller profile than Vanguard, faster, lower shield but higher agility. Solo ERT machine.

slots.label: Guns
slots.val: 2× S3 nose (fixed) + 4× S2 wing (fixed) — 6 total pilot guns

builds.label: ERT Meta Build
builds.weapons: 2× Deadbolt III (S3 nose) + 4× Deadbolt II (S2 wing)
builds.notes: The 6-gun convergence is the Lightning's identity. All 6 converge on a single point — devastating alpha. Open every ERT with all 8 missiles then sustained gun fire. S3 nose does the heavy lifting, S2 wing adds sustained DPS. FR-76 is the priority BP — get it from Foxwell Red tier.

builds.label: PvP Aggressive Build
builds.notes: Full energy — no ammo management. Sustain over alpha. Fly aggressively, use the small profile and agility advantage over heavier ships. Do not try to out-survive a Vanguard — out-manoeuvre it.

verdict: Pledge only in 4.8. If you have one it is the cleanest solo heavy fighter available — no extra crew, no compromises. 6-gun alpha burst is unmatched in the solo small ship category.

name: F7A Hornet Mk II
playstyle: Military variant of the F7C Mk II. Same loadout, upgraded internals, higher baseline DPS. Squadron 42 reward — limited in-game availability. Treat it like the F7C Mk II but better in every stat.

slots.label: Guns
slots.val: 2× S4 wing (fixed) + 1× S5 centre (fixed) — same as F7C Mk II

builds.label: PvE Meta — Deadbolt
builds.weapons: Deadbolt V (S5 centre) + 2× Deadbolt IV (S4 wing)
builds.notes: Identical build to the F7C Mk II but the F7A's internal stats are noticeably better at the same loadout. S5 Deadbolt V is the cornerstone — highest single-gun damage slot on the ship. Standard ERT meta.

builds.label: Full Energy Sustain
builds.notes: No ammo management for long session stacking. Military-grade internals mean this sustains better than the civilian Mk II at the same loadout.

verdict: If you have it, it is strictly better than the F7C Mk II. Build it identically — the advantage is in the airframe, not the loadout.

name: Guardian MX
playstyle: 4× S4 pilot guns on a solo heavy fighter frame. Dual M shields. High sustained DPS, excellent for ERT tier. New 4.8 ship — aggressive entry in the heavy fighter bracket.

builds.label: ERT Sustained Build
builds.missiles: 24× Ignite II (S2) — fire in salvos of 8
builds.notes: 4× S4 Deadbolts is a brutal sustained DPS package. 24 missiles means you can open 3 full salvos before running dry. The dual cooler setup (M + S1) is unusual — prioritise the M slot. Stack 8-10 ERT contracts and run the session.

builds.label: PvP Build
builds.missiles: Tempest II (S2 IR) × 12 — fire 2 racks at a time
builds.notes: Full energy sustain. 4× M4A is very consistent DPS with no ammo pressure. The heavy shield and relatively agile frame for its size makes it competitive vs medium fighters.

verdict: Strong new heavy fighter. 4× S4 pilot guns with M shield is a compelling package. Sits between the Vanguard Harbinger and F8C in capability.

name: Ares Ion
playstyle: Single S7 laser cannon. Extremely high alpha per shot — designed to crack capital ship armor. Poor vs agile fighters. Best used in org fleet ops as a capital-killer wing.

slots.label: Guns
slots.val: 1× S7 BEHR SF7E Laser Cannon (fixed — cannot swap)

builds.label: Capital Killer Build
builds.weapons: S7 Laser Cannon (stock — locked) + missiles only
builds.missiles: 12× Ignite V (S5) or Ignite IV — max damage class
builds.notes: The S7 laser is the entire point. Component upgrades are the only variable — maximise shield and power output. Fire the S7 in sustained bursts at capital-class targets. Against small fighters the single-gun design is a serious disadvantage — never solo dogfight in this ship. Needs fighter escort.

builds.label: Missile Harassment
builds.notes: Standoff role. Stay at range, fire S7 salvos from outside effective fighter range, use S5 missiles against larger targets. Keep a fighter escort between you and anything agile.

verdict: Specialist org ship — not a solo grinder. Devastating in fleet ops where fighters are covering it. Do not fly solo into a multi-target engagement.

name: Ares Inferno
playstyle: Single S7 ballistic Gatling. Highest sustained ballistic DPS of any fighter. Shreds armor at medium range. Heavy ammo dependency — manage run economy carefully.

slots.label: Guns
slots.val: 1× S7 BEHR SF7B Ballistic Gatling (fixed — cannot swap)

builds.label: ERT Armor Strip Build
builds.notes: The Gatling strips armor faster than any other pilot-operated gun in game. Use it at 500-800m range where spread is still tight. Ammo is the constraint — carry reload reserves and plan station returns into your contract stack. FR-76 shield is priority.

verdict: ERT armor stripper. Very effective at its job vs Inferno. Less versatile than the Ion but stronger vs armored multicrew ships at ERT tier. Needs fighter cover like the Ion.

name: Hurricane
playstyle: Budget heavy fighter. 2× S4 pilot + manned nose turret. Affordable at 9.23M aUEC. Good ERT capability, lower ceiling than Vanguard but much cheaper. Good entry point to heavy fighter tier.

slots.label: Turret (nose)
slots.val: 1× manned S4 nose turret (solo: pilot-controlled remote)

builds.label: Solo ERT Build
builds.weapons: 2× Deadbolt IV (S4 pilot) + Deadbolt IV (S4 nose remote)
builds.missiles: 4× Ignite III — small opener, follow with guns
builds.notes: Solo: nose turret runs in remote mode, pilot-controlled. Gives 3× S4 total effective DPS. Cheaper power/cooler are fine — S1 slots keep cost low. At 9.23M this is the most accessible heavy fighter entry. FR-76 M is the upgrade that matters most.

builds.label: Duo Build
builds.weapons: Pilot: 2× M4A (S4 energy) + gunner: Deadbolt IV (S4 nose)
builds.notes: With a gunner the nose turret becomes an independent gun — effectively a 3-gun combined platform. Gunner calls flankers. Very effective for VHRT/ERT duo grinding.

verdict: 9.23M for a heavy fighter with M shield and 3× S4 equivalent. The cheapest path to heavy fighter tier. Outclassed by Vanguard/F8C but costs a fraction of the price.

name: Esperia Stinger
playstyle: Tevarin-inspired heavy fighter. Unique 3-hardpoint layout: S5 + S4 + S2. High alpha potential with mixed-size guns. Fast for a heavy. Tevarin shield tech — different handling profile.

builds.label: Mixed Alpha Build
builds.weapons: Deadbolt V (S5) + Deadbolt IV (S4) + Deadbolt II (S2)
builds.notes: The asymmetric gun layout is unusual — the S5 does most of the work, S4 adds sustained, S2 is supplemental. Deadbolts across all three for consistent ballistic DPS. M shield on an agile medium frame is strong.

builds.label: Energy Sustain
builds.notes: Full energy — good for long session grinding. The S2 energy gun contribution is minor but sustain matters over long ERT stacks.

verdict: Strong heavy fighter for those who have it. Fast, good DPS, M shield. The S1 power/cooler slots are the weak point — they cap your component ceiling.

name: Redeemer
playstyle: 4.8 org gunship. 2× S4 pilot + 2× manned S4 turrets + 2× manned S3 turrets. 16× S2 missiles. Needs crew to justify — with 3-4 crew it is a devastating platform that tanks better than a Hammerhead per-gun.

slots.label: Turrets
slots.val: 2× manned S4 turrets + 2× manned S3 turrets

builds.label: Full Crew Org Build
builds.weapons: Pilot: 2× M4A S4 + turret gunners: Deadbolt IV (S4) × 2 + M3A (S3) × 2
builds.notes: With full crew this puts out 2× S4 pilot + 4 turret guns simultaneously. Combined DPS rivals capital-adjacent platforms. L-grade power/cooler despite being a medium ship — the internal volume is the Redeemer's secret. Assign roles before undocking: pilot, shield/power op, 2 turret gunners. Coordinator on comms.

builds.label: Solo/Duo Fallback
builds.notes: Solo it flies like a heavy fighter with a lot of wasted slots. Still viable for ERT due to M shield and good pilot DPS, but you are leaving 4 turret guns unused. Much better with even one gunner.

verdict: Org ship first, solo ship second. With 3-4 crew it is a terrifying platform. Solo it is competent but wasteful. Catfight org op ship — assign a regular crew.

name: Starlancer TAC
playstyle: Medium-class gunship with L-grade shield on a medium frame. 4× S4 pilot + manned turrets. Extremely tanky for its class. 4.8 new ship. Org gunship and ERT platform.

slots.label: Turrets
slots.val: 2× manned S4 turrets + 1× manned S3 belly turret

builds.label: ERT Tank Build
builds.weapons: 4× Deadbolt IV (S4 pilot) + turrets: Deadbolt IV × 2 + M3A S3
builds.shield: FR-76 L or Asclepius L (crafted shield BP priority)
builds.notes: 144,000 HP L-shield on a medium ship is extraordinary — this tanks more damage than most large ships. 4 pilot S4 guns is serious DPS before turrets even fire. Stack ERT contracts and absorb punishment that would kill any other medium fighter. Crew: 1 pilot, 1 shield/power op, 1-2 gunners.

builds.label: Solo ERT Build
builds.weapons: 4× M4A Laser Cannon (S4) — no ammo management solo
builds.notes: Solo viable thanks to the exceptional shield HP. Fly like a heavy fighter — absorb damage, stay on target, power into shields when needed. Turrets unmanned but the 4× S4 pilot guns are enough for ERT targets.

verdict: Arguably the best medium for tanking ERT hits. The L shield on a medium hull is absurd. Expensive pledge ship but if you have it, it changes how you approach combat — you stop worrying about taking hits.

name: C2 Hercules Starlifter
playstyle: Covalex Master grind ship. 696 SCU cargo. Also the Adagio Titanium endgame hauler. Drive-in ramp — load cargo vehicles directly. Self-sufficient with 2 S5 pilot guns + 2 turrets for defense.

builds.label: Covalex Master Grind
builds.qd: VK-00 L or XL-1 L (crafted QD — Covalex BP reward)
builds.notes: QD is the critical slot — VK-00 and XL-1 are Covalex Master BPs, fitting that you earn them on the way to grinding Master rank. Stack Courier + Hauling contracts simultaneously for 2× XP per trip. 696 SCU means almost every contract can be stacked in one load. Priority destination: Covalex Master at 237,750 XP.

builds.label: Adagio Titanium Loop
builds.notes: At Senior Contractor rank with a C2, the Adagio Titanium loop generates 4-5M+ aUEC/hr. Accept salvage contracts, fly to designated wrecks, scan for Titanium cargo boxes, steal and haul to sell. The C2 ramp makes loading titanium boxes significantly faster than smaller ships. This is the income ceiling for haulers in 4.8.

verdict: Hauling endgame. 18.9M is the big investment — worth every credit for org logistics and Covalex Master. Buy it after earning Covalex Senior rank so you already have income to cover it.

name: Zeus Mk II CL
playstyle: 128 SCU in a refined medium hull. 2× S4 pilot + turrets. Better handling than Freelancer MAX for the same hauling tier. Covalex Member to Senior grind ship. More accessible than C2 at 6.35M.

builds.label: Covalex Hauling Build
builds.shield: 3× Asclepius M — triple shield is the standout feature
builds.notes: The triple shield setup is unique — 3× M generator slots gives extraordinary shield HP for a medium hauler. Prioritise filling all 3 shield slots before other upgrades. Stack Covalex Courier + Hauling simultaneously. At 6.35M it is very reachable early and dramatically speeds up the Covalex rep grind.

builds.label: Light Combat Haul
builds.notes: With a gunner it can defend itself surprisingly well — triple shield + 4× S4 combined DPS. Use for high-value haul runs in contested space.

verdict: Best medium hauler for the price in 4.8. Triple shield is genuinely exceptional for a ship this size. Covalex grind ship of choice before you can afford a C2.

name: RSI Hermes
playstyle: 288 SCU in a medium hull. Covalex Senior interstellar grind ship. Cross-system hauls — Stanton to Pyro routes. 2 crew max. Fast for a medium hauler at 1200 SCM. Sensible upgrade from Freelancer MAX.

builds.label: Covalex Senior Build
builds.weapons: 2× M4A S4 laser (defensive — long sustain no ammo)
builds.qd: VK-00 M (crafted — Covalex BP) or Atlas M
builds.notes: At Covalex Senior rank you unlock interstellar hauls — cross-system runs between Stanton and Pyro. 288 SCU means you can stack multiple Senior contracts in one load. 180 SCM is fast enough that QT time per run is competitive with smaller ships. VK-00 QD is a Covalex BP unlock — fitting to equip it as you grind toward it.

builds.label: Pyro Run Build
builds.weapons: 2× Deadbolt IV (S4 ballistic) — ammo for pirate deterrence
builds.notes: Pyro runs are profitable but riskier. The Hermes can defend itself — 2× S4 + turrets if crewed. Fly indirect routes to avoid standard interdiction lanes.

verdict: Covalex Senior's natural ship. 288 SCU cross-system hauls at 7.11M is excellent value. Buy it when you hit Covalex Member rank and you will earn it back in a session.
